title: Cordless Tool Charging Station
summary: Cordless tools are great, but finding a place to keep their chargers and batteries can be a challenge.
description: Cordless tools are great, but finding a place to keep their chargers and batteries can be a challenge. This wall-mounted station offers space for several chargers, plus storage for three drills. It’s a great way to keep your cordless tools organized and ready to go.

steps.title: Make a Top Shelf
steps.body: Cut a Top Shelf to length from a 1x4 board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Set your Kreg® Pocket-Hole Jig for 3/4"-thick material, and then drill pocket holes in the Top Shelf, as shown. Sand the faces smooth.

steps.title: Add Top Shelf Trim
steps.body: Now cut a piece of Top Shelf Trim from a 1x2 board, as shown in the cutting diagram, and sand the faces smooth. Then attach the Trim to the Top Shelf using 1 1/4" coarse-thread Kreg Pocket-Hole Screws, as shown.

steps.title: Make a Top Rail
steps.body: Cut a Top Rail to length from a 1x6 board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Drill pocket holes in the Top Rail, as shown, and then sand the faces smooth.

steps.title: Attach the Top Rail
steps.body: Now you can attach the Top Shelf assembly to the Top Rail using 1 1/4" coarse-thread Kreg Pocket-Hole Screws.

steps.title: Make the Middle Shelf
steps.body: Cut a Middle Shelf to length from a 1x8 board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Drill pocket holes at the ends, as shown. Then lay out and drill three 2 1/2"-diameter holes where shown. You’ll be able to slip your drill chucks into these holes for storage. Sand the faces smooth.

steps.title: Attach the Middle Shelf
steps.body: Next, attach the Middle Shelf to the Top Shelf/Rail assembly using 1 1/4" coarse-thread Kreg Pocket-Hole Screws. Make sure that the ends of the all the pieces are flush, and sand them if needed.

steps.title: Make the Bottom Shelf Parts
steps.body: Cut a Bottom Shelf to length from a 1x8 board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Drill pocket holes at the locations shown. Then drill a half-circle cutout along the back edge of the Middle Shelf using a 2 1/2" hole saw, as shown. The easiest way to do this is to clamp a scrap piece along the edge of the shelf, then drill where the board meet. You can also cut the Bottom Shelf Trim from a 1x2 board. Then cut the profile at each end, as shown. Just stand the piece on edge on your miter saw, set the saw for a 45° miter, and trim off the corners.

steps.title: Assemble the Bottom Shelf
steps.body: Position the Bottom Shelf Trim, as shown, so that it overhangs the Bottom Shelf by 3/4" on each end, and so that the lower edge of the Trim is flush with the underside of the Shelf. Attach the Trim to the Bottom Shelf using 1 1/4" coarse-thread Kreg Pocket-Hole Screws.

steps.title: Create a Bottom Rail
steps.body: Cut a Bottom Rail to length from a 1x6 board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Drill pocket holes in the Bottom Rail, as shown, and then sand the faces smooth.

steps.title: Attach the Bottom Rail
steps.body: Now you can attach the Bottom Rail to the Bottom Shelf assembly using 1 1/4" coarse-thread Kreg Pocket-Hole Screws, as shown. Make sure that the ends of the Bottom Rail and the Bottom Shelf are flush, and sand them if needed.

steps.title: Make the Sides
steps.body: Cut two Sides to length from a 1x8 board, as shown in the cutting diagram. Then cut the angles on the ends, as shown. You can do this easily with a miter saw by setting the saw for a 45° cut. Sand the faces, edges, and ends smooth.

steps.title: Attach the Middle Shelf Assembly
steps.body: With the parts all made and the subassemblies together, you can begin final assembly. Position the Middle Shelf/Rail/Top Shelf assembly as shown, and then attach these pieces using 1 1/4" coarse-thread Kreg Pocket-Hole Screws.

steps.title: Add the Bottom Shelf Assembly
steps.body: Position the Bottom Shelf/Rail assembly, as shown, and attach it to the Side using 1 1/4" coarse-thread Kreg Pocket-Hole Screws.

steps.title: Add the Other Side
steps.body: To complete your charging station, attach the other Side, as shown. You can use the station as is, or you can add a coat of paint or finish. We wiped on a couple coats of oil finish. Then you can mount a power strip to the backside of the Bottom Rail. That way, you can plug your chargers into the power strip, rather than needing a separate wall outlet for each one. Finally, hang your charging station on the wall. If possible, locate it over a wall stud, and drive two 3" screws through the Top Rail and into the stud. If you can’t locate the station over a stud, use medium- or heavy-duty wall anchors.
